mnemonics: S.P.A.M. (Sum Principle: Add Mutually exclusive events, Product Principle: Multiply Multiple independent choices)
keyPoints: The Sum Rule is used when you have mutually exclusive choices (OR situations).
The Product Rule is used when you make a sequence of independent choices (AND situations).
The rules are fundamental for counting problems in combinatorics and probability.
commonMistakes: Using the Sum Rule for non-mutually exclusive events (forgetting to subtract the overlap).
Using the Product Rule for dependent events without adjusting for the dependency.
